description: Post-merge polish & real-Octave bug bash. The polish-v2 branch is merged into main. End-to-end validation against a real Octave 8.4 install found several bugs: download-octave.js URLs are broken (macOS 9.4 release doesn't exist, Linux AppImage repo doesn't exist), a few UX rough edges, and assorted cleanups. This cycle also lifts miscellaneous polish: make the editor Run button visibly indicate what it runs, add a busy indicator while Octave executes a long command, and land any remaining small cosmetics.

userStories.title: Fix broken URLs in scripts/download-octave.js
userStories.description: The download script references https://ftpmirror.gnu.org/octave/windows/... (returns 502), https://github.com/octave-app/octave-app/releases/download/v9.4.0/... (the 9.4.0 release doesn't exist; octave-app max is v9.2), and https://github.com/ryanjdillon/octave-appimage/releases/... (the ryanjdillon/octave-appimage repo doesn't exist). Pin to working sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows URL uses https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/octave/windows/ (mirror-free, confirmed 200 OK)
macOS URL pinned to v9.2 (latest octave-app release) or a working alternative — document the ceiling clearly

Linux URL replaced with a working source: either a maintained octave AppImage, a Flatpak bundle step (flatpak --user install org.octave.Octave), OR a clear 'not bundled on Linux' branch that falls back to PATH lookup

Running `node scripts/download-octave.js --platform linux` on a clean dev checkout either succeeds OR exits with a clear 'no bundled Octave for this platform, please install via apt/flatpak/brew' message (no HTTP 404 stacktrace)
Unit tests for download-octave updated to match the new URLs (tests/unit/download-octave*.test.ts)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Windows pinned to ftp.gnu.org/gnu/octave/windows/ (9.4.0). macOS pinned to octave-app v9.2 (release ceiling). Linux: bundling removed — script exits cleanly with package-manager instructions; runtime falls back to PATH. Tests updated.

userStories.title: Busy indicator while Octave executes a long command
userStories.description: When the user types a command that takes more than ~300ms (e.g. a tight loop, a large matrix op), the UI should visibly indicate 'running' so the user knows it didn't hang. Currently the status bar just stays on 'Ready' until the next status message.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar changes from 'Ready' to 'Running...' (or equivalent) whenever an octaveExecute IPC is in flight and takes longer than 250ms
Returns to 'Ready' when the execute resolves (output or error)
Nested executes (workspace refresh + command) don't cause flicker — a single ref-counted 'in-flight' state
A test that mocks a slow octaveExecute and asserts the status bar text sequence
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Ref-counted octaveBusyTracker in the renderer wraps window.matslop.octaveExecute. Status bar flips to 'Running…' after 250ms of sustained in-flight activity (no flicker on fast commands), ref-counts nested executes (workspace refresh + user cmd), and returns to Ready on settle. 11 unit tests in tests/unit/octave-busy-tracker.test.ts.

userStories.title: Stop button should cancel long-running execution, not crash Octave
userStories.description: Verify the editor toolbar Stop button interrupts a long-running command via SIGINT (existing octaveInterrupt IPC) and returns the user to a ready prompt without losing the Octave process.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Stop while a command is running sends SIGINT to Octave
Octave prints 'error: interrupted' or similar and returns to the prompt
Workspace state is preserved (variables defined before the interrupt remain)
Status bar returns to Ready
Test: mock a long execute, click stop, assert interrupt IPC was called
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Workspace panel shows scalar values truncated nicely
userStories.description: Matrix columns clip the 'Value' cell for anything non-scalar. Current preview formatValuePreview returns '[3x3 double]' which is fine, but scalars display inconsistently. Audit and unify the format.
acceptanceCriteria: Scalars show their numeric value truncated to 6 significant digits (e.g. 3.14159, 1.00000e-05)
Strings show 'abc...' truncated at 20 chars with ellipsis
Non-scalars show '[RxC class]'
Unit test for formatValuePreview covering scalar / string / matrix / struct / cell
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Editor Run button runs the whole open script via `run`/`source`
userStories.description: Currently clicking the Run (F5) button on an untitled buffer with a `function` definition produces no visible output. Running should execute the whole file via `source('<path>')` for saved files, or prompt to save the buffer first for untitled ones.
acceptanceCriteria: Run (F5) on a saved .m file calls octaveExecute(`source('<abs path>')`) and surfaces any output in the Command Window
Run on an unsaved buffer shows a 'Save before running' prompt, or writes to a temp file and sources that
Function-only files trigger a warning banner: 'This file only defines function(s); nothing to run'
Test covering saved/unsaved/function-only cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
